title: Aluminum Futures Rebound Amid Prolonged Supply Disruptions from Middle East
summary: Aluminum futures in the UK rebounded to $3,500 per tonne from a three-week low of $3,480 on April 29th, driven by expectations of ongoing supply disruptions from the Middle East. US President Trump indicated that the naval blockade on Iranian commercial vessels exiting the Strait of Hormuz would continue longer than anticipated. The price rose to 3,503.80 USD/T on May 8, up 0.59% from the previous day.

title: Comex Aluminium Premiums Beyond August Dip on US Tariff Rollback Bets
summary: Prices of aluminium premium contracts on Comex exchange beyond August have fallen due to bets on a US tariff rollback. This development reflects shifting expectations in the aluminum market amid potential policy changes. The current Aluminium futures price stands at 3,503.05, with a previous close of 3,483.23.

title: Aluminum rose to 3503.80 USD/T on May 8, 2026
summary: Aluminum prices rebounded to $3,500 per tonne amid expectations of prolonged supply disruptions from the Middle East. US President Trump signaled that the naval blockade would remain longer for Iranian commercial vessels, with aluminum futures hovering near a four-year high as the Strait of Hormuz blockage threatens continued supply disruptions.

title: America's Aluminum Supply Hits Zero in 30 Days
summary: Bloomberg has warned that American aluminum supplies could hit zero within 30 days due to a 50% tariff on Canadian aluminum that triggered supply chain disruptions. Production disruptions across the Middle East have left American manufacturers with almost no major foreign sources, with Bank of America warning the deficit could widen to 1.5 million tons.

title: Canada's MASSIVE Aluminum Discovery near Saskatchewan
summary: Canada's massive aluminum discovery near Saskatchewan could reshape global supply chains with billions of tonnes of resources. Early projections estimate the deposit could support mining operations for over 2,000 years at 50,000 tons per day, with potential to produce 1.88 million tons of aluminum annually.

title: Global Aluminium Production Slips in Q1 2026
summary: Global aluminium production declined 1.6% from 18.6 million tonnes in Q4 2025 to 18.3 million tonnes in Q1 2026. China's alumina production grew 8% in Q3 2025, reaching 22.7 million tonnes, signalling surplus pressure in the market.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,244 CNY/T on May 8, 2026, down 0.22% from the previous day, though up 5.63% over the past month and 6.40% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures surged to around CNY 3,250 per ton, the strongest since August last year, driven by robust buying in China post-May Day holiday and seven consecutive weeks of inventory drawdowns indicating tightening supply. Market sentiment is bolstered by strong seasonal demand and restocking.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel fell to 1,126.10 USD/T on May 8, 2026, down 0.43% from the previous day, despite reaching 1100.00 USD/T, the highest since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, prices gained 3.79%. The market shows ongoing upward trends amid broader steel price movements.

title: India's NMDC raises iron ore prices in May
summary: Indian state-controlled miner NMDC raised iron ore prices for the fourth straight month in May, setting 10-40mm Baila 65.5pc lump ore at 5,500 rupees/t ($58/t) and 64pc Baila fines at Rs4,700/t, up Rs200/t on the month. Prices are on a free-on-rail basis excluding taxes and fees, despite domestic steel prices facing pressure from slow demand. This reflects continued strength in iron ore amid steel market challenges.

title: Steel Industry Reps to Deliver Testimony in Section 301 Investigation
summary: Representatives of the U.S. steel industry are expected to testify about the detrimental effects of excess industrial capacity and production in 16 economies, including China, the EU, Mexico and Japan.

title: As Electricity Demand Increases, Steel Mills Hold a Big Bargaining Chip
summary: Industry executives discuss concerns that electric utilities may prioritize data center needs over steel mill power demands, though fears of sudden prioritization are likely unfounded.

title: Ramp-Up of New Ternium Lines Moving Quicker Than Expected
summary: Latin American steel producer Ternium is ramping up the new cold mill and galvanizing line at its Pesquería facility in Mexico faster than anticipated and expects to start the facility's new steel shop later this year.

title: Ferrosource Metal Processing Plant Approaching Completion at Big River Steel Works
summary: Ferrosource announced that its metal processing plant at United States Steel Corporation's Big River Steel Works in Osceola, Arkansas is approaching its scheduled completion in August 2026.

title: U.S. Steel Targets 2029 Start for DRI Plant
summary: United States Steel Corporation is allotting approximately 30 months for construction of its newly announced direct reduction plant and expects to bring the facility online in the first half of 2029.

title: Nucor Sees Record Shipments — And the End of West Virginia Mill Construction
summary: Nucor Corp. steel mills set an all-time shipping record during the first three months of 2026, and its new West Virginia sheet mill is entering the final phases of construction.

title: Domestic Raw Steel Production Week Ending May 2, 2026
summary: Domestic raw steel production reached 1,856,000 net tons with a capability utilization rate of 80.4 percent. Production was distributed across regions: North East (134k), Great Lakes (511k), Midwest (311k), Southern (838k), and Western (62k) net tons.

title: Copper futures remained above $5.95 per pound on Friday after rebounding in the previous session
summary: Copper futures stayed above $5.95 per pound on Friday, supported by signs of improving demand in China. This follows a rebound in the previous session despite ongoing headwinds from the Middle East conflict. The price rose to 6.25 USD/Lbs on May 8, up 2.04% from the previous day.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The announcement comes amid rising copper prices.

title: US imposes sanctions on Congo armed group, mining firms over illicit minerals
summary: The United States announced sanctions against an armed group and mining firms in Congo related to illicit mineral operations. This action impacts the copper supply chain in a major producing region.

title: China state firm discusses major new copper mine in Congo
summary: China Railway Group is proposing a major new copper mine project in Congo expected to produce between 200,000 and 500,000 tons of copper annually. This represents significant capacity expansion in African copper production.

title: Copper supply under pressure: Should prices be higher?
summary: Multiple top-tier copper mines are facing ongoing supply disruptions, creating tight supply-demand dynamics. Frazer Tabeart from Alma Metals discusses how supply risks are amplifying price pressures in the copper market.

title: Supply disruptions at copper mines driving higher prices
summary: Mining analyst Sam Crittenden from RBC Capital Markets discusses the copper bull run driven by supply disruptions at major mines. The analysis covers current market dynamics and preferred copper stocks.

title: Copper Prices Reach Record Amid Supply Squeeze and Tariff Fears
summary: Copper prices have reached record levels as multiple mines experience supply disruptions and tariff concerns emerge. Industrial activity and shipping dynamics are influencing market conditions.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: Explores how new technologies and favorable policies can bridge the looming copper supply-demand gap driven by the energy transition. Discusses the critical role of copper in renewable energy infrastructure, electric vehicles, and other applications, with projections showing demand growing from 26 million tonnes in 2024 to 35 million tonnes by 2035.

title: Copper's quiet supply squeeze is reshaping the investment case
summary: Analyzes the structural supply deficit in copper driven by electrification, AI, and declining ore grades. Reports that global copper demand is projected to reach 42 million metric tons by 2040, while production is expected to peak in 2030 and decline thereafter, creating a potential 10 million metric ton deficit by 2040.

title: Technology Minerals unlocks new innovation and revenue stream with copper and aluminium recovery
summary: Highlights Technology Minerals' innovation in copper and aluminium recovery from batteries, creating new revenue streams. Focuses on sustainable recovery solutions for critical minerals.

title: Copper's transformation into a strategic resource in the AI and energy transition
summary: Examines copper's emerging role as a strategic resource critical to AI infrastructure, clean energy, and grid upgrades. Emphasizes the importance of copper for both economic development and national security.

title: Thanks to AI, copper demand will soar 50 per cent by 2040
summary: Reports that growth in artificial intelligence and defence sectors will drive a 50 percent increase in global copper demand by 2040. Notes that supplies are expected to fall significantly short of this projected demand.

title: Nickel Futures Surge Near 2-Year High
summary: Nickel futures traded above $19,500 per tonne, nearing a two-year high, driven by supply disruptions and tightening raw material availability. Mining quota cuts in Indonesia have constrained ore supply, while a sulfur shortage linked to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz has raised processing costs for nickel intermediates.

title: Nickel Prices Pull Back from Recent Rally as Profit-Taking Emerges
summary: Nickel fell to $18,878 USD/T on May 8, 2026, down 1.24% from the previous day, as traders locked in gains after the recent sharp rally. The pullback reflects profit-taking rather than deterioration in underlying fundamentals, with broader industrial metals showing mixed price action.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: Nickel futures are currently trading at $18,906.50, down from a previous close of $19,083.25. Despite recent gains, UBS has warned of a persistent surplus in the global nickel market through 2026.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has ordered the world’s largest nickel mine, PT Weda Bay Nickel, to significantly cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026, aiming to support global nickel prices and manage supply. The move highlights growing tensions between Indonesia’s export ambitions and global market dynamics for nickel used in electric vehicles and stainless steel.

title: Nickel breakthrough: EV batteries become 84% greener with new zero-carbon process
summary: A new nickel extraction process enables single‑step recovery from low‑grade ores with up to 84% lower emissions, offering a cleaner pathway for nickel used in electric vehicle batteries. The technology could significantly reduce the carbon footprint of EV supply chains while improving efficiency in nickel production.

title: Cogne closes 2024 in red despite higher shipments
summary: After a challenging 2023, Cogne Acciai Speciali continued to face headwinds in 2024, primarily in Italy, with its financial and operational performance further impacted by persistent market pressure. Despite higher shipments, the company closed the year with negative financial results.

summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali has activated solidarity contracts for 166 workers in response to an order drop and limited redundancy fund availability. Over three hundred employees at Cogne have been affected by the challenging market conditions.

summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali is implementing initiatives to reset its carbon footprint as part of sustainability efforts. The company continues to focus on environmental responsibility in its specialty steel operations.

title: Tenaris Board Appoints Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer
summary: Tenaris announced that its Board of Directors appointed Gabriel Podskubka as Chief Executive Officer. Paolo Rocca will continue to serve as Chairman of the Board.

title: Tenaris Awards Roberto Rocca Scholarships to Saudi Engineering Students
summary: Tenaris has awarded three Roberto Rocca Scholarships to engineering students from King Fahd University of Petroleum & Minerals in Saudi Arabia, reinforcing its commitment to developing local engineering talent in the Kingdom.

title: Tenaris Publishes 2025 Sustainability Statement
summary: Tenaris has published an executive summary of its 2025 Sustainability Statement included in its annual report, outlining how sustainability is embedded in its industrial strategy amid a complex geopolitical and market environment.

title: Tenaris Receives Ninth Consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions Recognition
summary: Tenaris receives ninth consecutive worldsteel Sustainability Champions recognition, demonstrating ongoing commitment to sustainable practices in steel production.
